rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF WILLIAM ESTRADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/23. (No earlier availability.) (1) No proof of service of notice of the second amended petition has been filed. (2) The only correct form of notice for a conservatorship petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-020, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (3) In any event the "proof of service" attached to this pleading purports to be proof of service of "Conservator's Response to Petition for Compensation," etc., which is not the title of this pleading. (4) Additionally, 10/9/23 was not at least 15 days before this hearing, so notice, even if the proper form of notice of this "Second Amended Petition to Close," etc., had been mailed on or after the date the petition was filed, it would have been defective. (5) The conservator was appointed on 4/29/21, which is the date on which assets of the conservatorship estate must be appraised. Period. [Probate Code Section 2610(a).] This is an accounting by the conservator, not a personal representative of a deceased person's estate. (6) It is unclear which of the three Corrected or Final or Corrected Final I&As, all verified on 10/11/23 and filed on 10/13/23, is the actual, correct, final I&A. (An accounting should NOT be prepared until an appraisal by a probate referee has been completed and filed. Paragraph 14 claims that the beginning assets "in May, 2021" was $291,923, but not one of the I&As agrees with that amount, and the conservator was appointed on 4/29/21.) (7) It appears that the conservator is still accounting for each bank account separately, but the beginning value of each account does not agree with any amount on an I&A, and none of the separate accountings has a schedule of assets on hand at the end of the accounting period, as required by Probate Code Section 1061(a)(10). For non-cash assets, the schedule of assets on hand should also include the estimated market value of each separate security. [Probate Code Section 1063(a).] (8) If there were sales of securities or other changes, such as reinvested dividends, they must be shown on separate GAINS or LOSSES schedules or on an informational schedule, not as "profit" on a receipts schedule or losses or "reinvested shares" on a disbursements schedule. The reinvested dividends or gains or losses on sales must also clearly indicate to which holding such transactions applied. Deadline is 12/1/23 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/23. (No earlier availability.) 1. This third amended petition was not filed until 10/6/23 and not e-filed until 10/9/23, neither of which date was at least 15 days before this hearing. 2. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14. A.] 3. The I&A filed on 9/22/22 showed assets on hand as of the date of death to be $205,146.01, not $205,095.88 as now alleged in paragraph 14. 4. Paragraph 22 says that the residue of the estate is to be distributed "as per exhibit 'B'", but Exhibit B shows distribution to two individuals, not to the trustee of the devisee trust. 5. The executor guesses that the probate referee charged an "administrative fee". Why does he not know? If all assets were cash, why was the probate referee involved at all? Was the referee commission paid? 6. Paragraph 25 still says that notice WAS GIVEN to the DCVC 7. Paragraphs 5 and 6 of the prayer refer to erroneous sections above. Deadline is 11/28/23 for providing the examiner a courtesy copy of (1) an amendment to the third amended petition and (2) proof of service of at least 15 days' notice of the 12/12/23 hearing of the third amended petition and amendment thereto, showing that notice was served after the amendment was e-filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. This third amended petition was not, in fact, fully amended to correct the myriad errors in the previous pleadings, including, among many typographical errors not mentioned here: (1) Letters TESTAMENTARY, not of Administration, were issued; (2) there is no accounting, as accounting was waived, and if there had been an accounting, the period of the accounting should have been set forth (not just "through the present date"); (3) the pleading is still missing the REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY of the party for whom the attorney is appearing. Please see Probate Code Section 1061 for how a Summary of Account should appear. Exhibit A to this pleading is just a list of assets without any dates.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILITINI MAUGATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The Court's 07/20/2022 order that appointed the administrator required her bond to be fixed as soon as assets of the estate were ascertained, but no bond has been fixed and filed since the administrator reportedly received the first asset in August 2022 and filed a final inventory and appraisal in November 2022. 2) Rasela Maugata is the only known child of the decedent at the outset of this case. During administration, Mikaio Maugata and Devin Cast were identified as the decedent's additional children by the Department of Child Support Services in Sacramento and Butte Counties that filed creditor claims for payment of court-ordered child support owed by the decedent to those two children. The child support order for Mikaio Maugata issued by the Sacramento County Superior Court states that there is a judgment in another county regarding Amilia Iosua referred to as the decedent's child. However, the administrator requests final distribution of the estate to Rasela Maugata alone, to the exclusion of the three other children who were identified during administration. No petition has been filed under Probate Code section 11700 to confirm or deny entielement of the decedent's additional children. 3) The record shows $637.50 as waived court fees. No reimbursement has been made from the estate cash on hand. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WENDY WAN GIN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The prayer seeks a $5,000 reserve, not $10,000. (2) Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] Detailed schedules are not lump sums of "interest, dividends, and other credits" received quarterly. (3) What happened to the assets held in the 401(k) accounts? It appears that they were liquidated if there is only cash on hand, and the cash is less than half the value of the appraised estate. (Paragraph 25.b. of the petition does not appear to be quite accurate.) (4) Transfer of cash between accounts is NOT a change in the form of assets. (Cash is cash.) [Probate Code Section 1063(b). (5) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Deadline is 12/1/23 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. (Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B.1.] Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] This Court has not required the Declaration of Real Property for several years. The e-filing fee should not have been incurred; however, in light of Counsel's mailing courtesy copies instead of using a more costly (unnecessary) messenger service, that e-filing cost will be allowed in this instance.)

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To File Under Seal: 1) The Executrix Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Early Release Of Funds, And 2) Order If Approved; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Gener D. Benitez In Support (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] Executor has not presented specific facts showing that the litigation parties' or the Trust's privacy interests outweigh the public interest. (See Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 2.550(d)(2).) Nor has the Executor presented specific facts to show a substantial probability exists that the Trust's interests would be prejudiced if the settlement's terms are not sealed. The motion does not identify "other contestants" who would file litigation allegedly under "the belief that they can get paid" by doing so, or that the Trust faces "endless litigation" if the terms of the settlement are not sealed. Nor has the Executor shown that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored or that it is the least restrictive means to protect the Trust's purported interest. (See Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 2.550(d)(4) & (5).) The proposed sealing, in fact, is not at all tailored; instead, the Executor seeks to shelter from public view the entire agreement, the Petition's summary of its terms, and the amount that must be transferred out of the estate and into the related Trust under the terms of the settlement. The parties to the settlement attended a Mandatory Settlement Conference under the court's July 5, 2023 order, and reached the instant settlement agreement (see, Motion at 2:14-19). Although the parties' attendance at the conference was ordered by the court, the terms of the settlement are the product of the parties' efforts alone. It was the parties' choice to agree to the terms of a settlement that requires court approval of an "early" partial release of funds from the estate to the Trust, prior to an order on final distribution and close of estate. It was also the parties' choice to stipulate to the court retaining jurisdiction to enforce this settlement agreement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6. For the court to retain jurisdiction over enforcement of a settlement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, the parties must meet the formal requirements under Code Civ. Proc., section 664.6 (see, Mesa RHF Partners, L.P. v. City of Los Angeles (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 913 and Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal. App. 4th 429.) Importantly, the statute vests the court alone with the power to grant or decline a request to retain jurisdiction over a given and specific settlement agreement: "[] If requested by the parties, the court may retain jurisdiction over the parties to enforce the settlement until performance in full of the terms of the settlement." (Civ. Proc. Sec. 664.6, emphasis added.) If the parties to the agreement want the court to consider approving the instant settlement agreement and allowing the "early" distribution of estate assets, then the parties to the agreement must accept that the court may require that it be made available on the public record to allow fulsome review and broad participation in the adjudicative process. In this case, the court has reviewed the agreement and finds that meaningful consideration of both the settlement agreement and the request for leave to distribute funds out of the estate "early" will require discussion of the agreement's terms at the scheduled hearing. [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MYRTLE KING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To File Under Seal: 1) The Executrix Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Early Release Of Funds, And 2) Order If Approved; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Gener D. Benitez In Support (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] This motion and the underlying Petition will both be discussed at noticed and open hearings; meaning, the material terms of the agreement will soon be a matter of public record. The administration of this Estate is yet to a close. Any accounting petitions and petitions for final distribution, or for extraordinary attorney's fees incurred due to the now-settled litigation efforts, will likely involve discussion of the settlement's terms and any court-approved "early" partial distribution of estate assets. In practice, sealing the terms of the settlement agreement, portions of the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement and Early Release of Funds to Pay Settlement, and part of any order issued regarding the Petition to Approval Settlement Agreement and Early Release of Funds to Pay Settlement, would be futile at best, and at worse, heavily encumber completion of the estate administration in compliance with statute (see. e.g. Prob. C. 10830 et seq., 10951, 11000 et seq., 11600 et seq.12200 et seq.) Executor shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MYRTLE KING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To File Under Seal: 1) The Executrix Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Early Release Of Funds, And 2) Order If Approved; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Gener D. Benitez In Support
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Pending in this department are Executor's Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement and Early Release of Funds to Pay Settlement, and Executor's Motion to Seal both the Petition and any order granting it. Executor seek an order sealing a summary of the terms of the settlement agreement and the settlement agreement itself. The Motion to Seal is DENIED. A court may order that a record be filed under seal only if it expressly finds facts that establish all of the following: (1) an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and, (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 2.550(d)(1)-(5); NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (Locke) (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1216-1218 ("NBC Subsidiary").) The party seeking the sealing of a record bears the burden of presenting information sufficient to identify the specific information claimed to be entitled to such treatment and the nature of the harm threatened by disclosure. (H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) The moving party must set forth specific facts in support of a motion to seal; statements that are too conclusory, abstract, or generic are not sufficient to satisfy the burden. (Id. at pp. 895-896.) First Amendment principles require that substantive courtroom proceedings in ordinary civil cases are "presumptively open." (Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 2.550(c) [court records presumed to be open].) The public's right to access to court records is fundamental to our constitutional system. (See NBC Subsidiary, supra, 20 Cal.4th 1216-1217.) The right to access is heightened in probate proceedings, where broad notice and opportunity to object is fundamental. (See, e.g., Estate of Stevenson (2006) 141 Cal.App.4th 1074, 1087-88 ["Notice is the cornerstone of estate proceedings. [Citations.]"].) Notice is lacking when access to the document submitted for judicial approval is withheld from interested parties. An open discussion assumes access to the record. The motion offers no authority to support the additional request that an order granting the underlying Petition redact the settlement amount approved for "early release" from the estate. Executor's argument in the motion that "[t]he Trust wants the terms of the settlement confidential because it does not want similar lawsuits against the Trust by other contestants to the belief that they can get paid if they sue the Trust. The Trust does not want endless litigation that would exhaust Trust funds" does not ameliorate the loss of access or address the statutory requirements of Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 2.550. (Motion at 3:11-14.) The motion does not set forth specific facts in support of finding that any of the five required elements have been established. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRI MAURICE JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the date of death alleged in the petition was incorrect, an amendment to the petition or a fully amended petition must be filed. (A supplement provides additional or missing information; it does not allege facts that differ from the facts alleged in the modified pleading. [CRC 7.3.]) (2) Notice of the amendment or fully amended petition must be mailed and published. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) If the petitioner is acting as the agent for the heirs-at-law, the durable powers of attorney must be filed. (Declinations and waivers of bond by the agent for both heirs and the agent's nominations of the petitioner should also be filed.) (5) A guardian ad litem may need to be appointed if the petitioner is also the attorney-in-fact. (6) A declaration or attachment to the petition, setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate, will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond for a proposed non-resident personal representative. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/1/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HELENE RENE GLIEBERMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petitioner is not one of the nominated executors. This should be a Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. (2) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete. (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) When did the decedent's parents and brother die? [LRSF 14.18 C.] (5) Bond is required of a non-resident personal representative. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (6) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] The instructions must be attached to the notice that is served. Deadline is 11/21/23 for providing the assigned examiner the proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
